description: Registry that tells Atlas which folders to surface and how. See atlas_registry_schema.md for the schema, atlas_spec.md for philosophy, atlas_classification.md for categories, atlas_v1_plan.md for v1 scope.

entries.title: Archive
entries.summary: Old texts, edited with care so they can be read.
entries.notes: v1 flagship world. Source folder is named 'Digital Archive'; public world name is 'Archive'. Authoring has migrated to workspace-hub/archive/ (see workspace-hub/archive/SYNC.md). local_authoring=true tells sync_atlas.py to leave the destination canonical.

entries.title: Language
entries.summary: An early study desk for sound, script, and language history.
entries.notes: v1 flagship world. Authoring is canonical at projects/language/ (Phonos study desk, see PROJECT.md). Public-facing copy at workspace-hub/language/ is mirrored manually; local_authoring=true tells sync_atlas.py not to touch it.

entries.title: Gods
entries.summary: An early comparative archive of pantheons — gods set beside one another across time and place.
entries.notes: v1 flagship world. The app and data live canonically at workspace-hub/gods/map/. The projects-side folder holds historical build scripts and the source for index.md only. local_authoring=true tells sync_atlas.py not to overwrite the deploy.

entries.title: Arabic
entries.summary: Short reading notes inside the Phonos language world.
entries.notes: Lives inside the Phonos study desk as a journal page; not a standalone world.

entries.title: IPA
entries.summary: The IPA chart — currently the home page of the Phonos language world.
entries.notes: The chart is not a separate subworld — it is the Phonos home. Entry kept for reference but hidden from sync; remove in a later registry cleanup.

entries.title: Digital Symbols
entries.notes: Deferred per Phonos PROJECT.md §7. Not a v1 surface; entry kept for reference but hidden from sync.

entries.title: Graph
entries.summary: The texts of the Archive, seen as a single structural map.
entries.notes: Static export from Atlas core. Refresh by re-running the pipeline and copying the JSON via tools/sync_atlas_graph.py.
